cleanmac is an AI-first, zero-resident macOS cleanup CLI and MCP-ready execution kernel.

Use cleanmac when an AI Host, script, or user needs dry-run-first macOS cleanup plans, auditable review handoff, safe Trash-based execution, or machine-readable cleanup evidence. cleanmac runs only when explicitly invoked and exits after the requested workflow. It must not be described or extended as a GUI cleaner, TUI app, menu bar utility, background optimizer, login item, daemon, automatic scanner, or user-retention surface.

Primary AI entry points:
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json capabilities`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json workflow --categories trash,downloads --dry-run-scope selected`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json ai-workflow`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json ai-host-integration-pack`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json ai-host-preflight`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json ai-host-evidence`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json release-readiness`
- `python3 cleanmac.py --json explain --input-file <plan-or-report>`

Execution safety:
- Cleanup defaults to dry-run.
- Real deletion requires explicit `clean run --execute`.
- Live root `/` requires `--allow-live-root`.
- Governed execution should preserve Trash routing, review-selection constraints, `--require-plan-context`, confirmation tokens, and operation logs.
- Destructive MCP tools must deny auto-call and require confirmation.
- Tests and validation should use `CLEANMAC_TEST_MODE=1` and `CLEANMAC_TEST_NO_AUTH=1`.

Important files:
- `AGENTS.md` is the contributor and AI-agent operating agreement.
- `aiflow.yaml` is the root-level aiflow workflow policy and command gate.
- `.aiflow/` is local-only runtime space for generated run evidence, store files, locks, scratch data, and temporary state. Do not place committed workflow policy or source files there.
- `docs/reference/contracts.md` summarizes stable JSON, AI, MCP, and execution contracts.
- `cleancli/delete_ops.py` is the only low-level deletion exit.
- `cleancli/ai_schema.py`, `cleancli/ai_versioning.py`, and `scripts/cleanmac_mcp_server.py` own AI/MCP tool contracts.
- `Makefile` owns local and release smoke gates.

Preferred validation commands:
- `make pytest-test`
- `make local-test`
- `make docs-smoke`
- `make governance-smoke`
- `make ai-governance-smoke`
- `make mcp-smoke`
- `make ai-host-smoke`
- `make release-readiness-smoke`
